title: Israeli Settlers Beat Palestinian Woman in Sa'ir, Attack Farmers Near Halhoul — West Bank
detail: On Saturday, April 19, Israeli settlers beat a Palestinian woman in Sa'ir village near Hebron, and separately, Israeli security forces stopped Palestinians from reaching their farmland in Halhoul (south of Hebron) after settlers reportedly pepper-sprayed local farmers working their land. The incidents were part of ongoing settler violence that escalated during the Iran war period, with settlers becoming 'increasingly emboldened' according to Palestinian rights organizations. IDF said it was 'looking into the matter' but made no arrests by evening.

media.caption: Israeli settlers beat Palestinian woman in Sa'ir, West Bank; farmers also attacked near Halhoul

title: 'I Thought I Might Die': Palestinian Mother Recounts Israeli Detention
detail: A Palestinian mother gave a firsthand account of her detention by Israeli forces in the West Bank, describing fear for her life and detailing the conditions she experienced. The testimony adds to growing documentation of detention practices by Israeli authorities in the occupied West Bank.

media.caption: Palestinian mother recounts her Israeli detention experience
